sources: docs/spec/AUCTION.md Winner Bond And Maturity / Prototype constant to resolve lines 179-183 [candidate]
docs/core/DECISIONS.md 13. Prototype maturity schedule status lines 187-199

inputs.attackFlagText: STATUS marks the value 'placeholder / test override' — a test override path leaking into the kernel would make maturity environment-dependent; the CONSENSUS_PARAMS surface must carry the only value.
inputs.predicateSignature: settlement(winningBid, MATURITY_BLOCKS[launch-freeze CONSENSUS_PARAM], witnessedChainFacts, priorKernelState) -> {verdict, maturityHeight}

params.MATURITY_BLOCKS: launch-freeze placeholder (STATUS = 52,560), carried as the sole CONSENSUS_PARAMS input

fixture.caseEpochHalvingOrOverride: a fixture deriving maturity from the legacy epoch-halving maturity-helper, or from any test-override path, so that it differs from the fixed MATURITY_BLOCKS parameter -> reject
fixture.positiveControl: maturity sourced solely from MATURITY_BLOCKS, uniform for every settled name -> accept
fixture.note: parameterized on MATURITY_BLOCKS as a launch-freeze placeholder, NOT on its value

inputs.scopeNote: Ratified via #66 (PR-10/11 settlement-finality/maturity; AUCTION + STATUS 'epoch-halving = prototype residue to remove/quarantine' + DECISIONS #13): the kernel sources maturity SOLELY from the fixed MATURITY_BLOCKS CONSENSUS_PARAM — no environment/test-override input and no epoch-halving/variable schedule. The parameter VALUE remaining a launch-freeze placeholder does not affect this fail-closed-against-override rule (parameterized, two values, NOT asserted). Authored at candidate tier.
inputs.authoringStatus: authored: predicate-input shape proposed at candidate tier; MATURITY_BLOCKS launch-freeze; reviewer sign-off complete; locked. Promoted candidate→ratified 2026-06-15 (#66, DK event 43d30e67) via PR-10/11 (rule ratified; maturity value remains launch-freeze).

expected.invariant: maturity is the single fixed MATURITY_BLOCKS parameter applied uniformly; a maturity derived from a test-override path or an epoch-halving/variable schedule is rejected
expected.rationale: This flag is about the fail-closed posture against a test-override / epoch-halving maturity input leaking into the kernel. The kernel-purity duty (uniform fixed MATURITY_BLOCKS parameter, no environment-dependent or epoch-halving schedule) is derivable from AUCTION.md and STATUS (epoch-halving = 'prototype residue to remove or quarantine') and DECISIONS #13; the parameter VALUE remaining a launch-freeze placeholder does not block a negative vector asserting no test-override/epoch-halving input exists. Ratified via #66 (PR-10/11; the rule is ratified, the maturity value stays launch-freeze).

sources: docs/core/DECISIONS.md 12. Maturity duration binding [decision log] lines 170-185
docs/core/DECISIONS.md 37. Bond opens the auction [ratified] line 575

inputs.attackFlagText: Without this rule a bidder picks its own maturity (settlementLockBlocks=1 -> instant mature owner, gutting the year-locked-capital deterrent of bond-opens (#37)).

fixture.caseShortLock: a winning bid carrying settlementLockBlocks=1 (or any value != MATURITY_BLOCKS) -> reject (MUST NOT settle into ownership with a shortened maturity)
fixture.positiveControl: settlementLockBlocks == MATURITY_BLOCKS -> settles (the field is a commitment validated against the protocol parameter, not a bidder-chosen override)
fixture.priorState: live auction with this as the winning bid

inputs.scopeNote: Cited-now (DECISIONS #12 deterministic maturity 'cannot be adjusted discretionarily' + normative WIRE_FORMAT §4.3 settlementLockBlocks field): a winning bid whose settlementLockBlocks != MATURITY_BLOCKS must not settle with a shortened maturity; the per-bid field is a commitment to validate, never a bidder-chosen override. The bond-opens (#37) year-locked-capital deterrent referenced is RATIFIED, not provisional.
inputs.authoringStatus: authored: predicate-input shape proposed; reviewer sign-off complete; locked.

expected.invariant: a winning bid whose settlementLockBlocks differs from the protocol MATURITY_BLOCKS parameter does not settle into ownership; settlementLockBlocks is a validated commitment, not a bidder-chosen maturity override
expected.rationale: S5 was upgraded to cited per DECISIONS #12 (deterministic maturity from launch rules; 'cannot be adjusted discretionarily'). A winning bid carrying settlementLockBlocks != MATURITY_BLOCKS must not settle with a shortened maturity. This negative verdict is derivable from #12 plus the normative WIRE_FORMAT field; the deterrent reference to bond-opens (#37) is RATIFIED, not provisional.

sources: docs/LAUNCH.md Bond Continuity Consequences / Bonds And Maturity

inputs.attackFlagText: Continuity is an ONT rule, not a Bitcoin timelock (GLOSSARY) — the kernel can only assign consequences to observed spends; the bond is spendable by whoever holds the funding wallet key, which is distinct from the owner key (Decision #41), so a funding-wallet compromise releases the name without any owner-key signature.

fixture.casePreMaturitySpendNoSuccessor: a pre-maturity spend of the name's bond outpoint with NO same-tx valid successor bond releases the name -> reject (ownership continuity broken), regardless of which key (funding-wallet, owner, attacker) signed the Bitcoin spend
fixture.property: no owner-signature exemption path exists; continuity is a consequence of an OBSERVED spend, not a key-authorized event
fixture.acceptPath: the sole exception (not this vector): a spend by a kernel-validated transfer/recovery that creates a valid successor bond in the same transaction
fixture.priorState: settled name, pre-maturity, bond outpoint unspent

inputs.scopeNote: Ratified via #66 (#56 settlement-bond-continuity / PR-23; LAUNCH Bond Continuity + AUCTION + ONT_ACQUISITION_STATE_MACHINE + GLOSSARY 'continuity is an ONT rule not a Bitcoin timelock'): release fires on a bond-outpoint spend with no same-tx valid successor, regardless of signing key — the kernel assigns consequences to observed spends with no owner-signature exemption. DECISIONS #41 (funding key distinct from owner key) is RATIFIED; the funding-key-compromise-releases-the-name is an ACCEPTED PROPERTY of bond continuity, not a missing rule. Authored at candidate tier.
inputs.authoringStatus: authored: predicate-input shape proposed at candidate tier; reviewer sign-off complete; locked. Promoted candidate→ratified 2026-06-15 (#66, DK event 43d30e67) via #56 / PR-23.

expected.invariant: a pre-maturity bond-outpoint spend with no same-tx valid successor releases the name (ownership continuity broken) regardless of which key signed the spend; there is no owner-signature exemption; release is an observed-spend consequence
expected.rationale: S6 is cited. The flag's substance — that release fires on a bond-outpoint spend with no same-tx valid successor regardless of which key (funding-wallet, owner) signed it (DECISIONS #41 keeps the funding key distinct from the owner key) — is exactly the rule: the kernel assigns consequences to observed spends, with no owner-signature exemption. This verdict is derivable from LAUNCH Bond Continuity + AUCTION + ONT_ACQUISITION_STATE_MACHINE + GLOSSARY. #41 is ratified, not provisional. The funding-key 'compromise' is an accepted property of bond continuity, not a missing rule. Ratified via #66 (#56/PR-23; authored at candidate).

sources: docs/spec/AUCTION.md Bond Breaks And Reauction lot-identity note lines 204-207

inputs.attackFlagText: The doc states the rule in adapter voice ('The indexer only recognizes') — promotion must restate it as a pure kernel predicate over (bid bytes, recorded release facts), or an indexer remains the deciding party.

fixture.caseStaleAnchor: a bid whose lot commitment binds unlockBlock = a stale earlier release height -> reject (does not open/join the live generation)
fixture.caseFabricatedFuture: a bid binding a fabricated future release height -> reject
fixture.caseZeroAfterRelease: a bid with unlockBlock=0 (first-gen anchor) presented after a release -> reject
fixture.positiveControl: unlockBlock == the latest recorded release height -> opens the new generation, lot identity reopen-{name}-after-{release_height}; a genuine first-generation auction carries anchor 0 with identity opening-{name}
fixture.priorState: a name with a recorded bond-break release height

inputs.scopeNote: Ratified via #66 (#56 settlement-bond-continuity / PR-28 auction-id grammar; LAUNCH Released-Name Reauction Path + AUCTION lot-identity note): a reopened auction is recognized ONLY if its lot anchor unlockBlock equals the latest recorded release height. WIRE_FORMAT §6 (normative) is the COMMITMENT ENCODING source only — it defines the auctionLotCommitment / unlockBlock preimage field, NOT the reauction verdict. The flag is about restating adapter voice ('the indexer recognizes') as a pure kernel predicate over (bid bytes, recorded release facts) — verdict unchanged; the predicate restatement is the standard promotion this vector exercises (removes indexer-as-decider). Authored at candidate tier (an earlier overstated-normative tier was a reviewer catch, event 4c87b9ae).
inputs.authoringStatus: authored: predicate-input shape proposed; reviewer sign-off complete; locked. Promoted candidate→ratified 2026-06-15 (#66, DK event 43d30e67) via #56 / PR-28.

expected.invariant: a bid whose lot commitment binds any anchor other than the latest recorded release height (stale, fabricated-future, or unlockBlock=0 after a release) does not open or join the live generation; only unlockBlock == release height opens reopen-{name}-after-{release_height}
expected.rationale: The reopen-anchor verdict (a reopened auction is recognized only if its lot anchor unlockBlock equals the latest recorded release height, lot identity reopen-{name}-after-{release_height}; first-gen anchor 0) is derived from LAUNCH Released-Name Reauction Path + AUCTION lot-identity note. WIRE_FORMAT §6 (normative) supplies only the auctionLotCommitment/unlockBlock ENCODING, not the verdict authority. The flag is only about restating adapter voice ('the indexer recognizes') as a pure kernel predicate over (bid bytes, recorded release facts) — the verdict itself is unchanged and derivable; the predicate restatement is the standard promotion the vector already exercises. Ratified via #66 (#56/PR-28; authored at candidate, after the reviewer catch event 4c87b9ae corrected an initially overstated normative tier).

sources: docs/spec/AUCTION.md Winner Bond And Maturity (Maturity reset on transfer | No) line 172 [candidate]
docs/LAUNCH.md Transfers (maturity clock does not reset)

inputs.attackFlagText: No-reset means a buyer near maturity inherits an almost-free name lock — combined with S3's bid-height anchor ambiguity, transfer timing games could shave the effective lock; the X* extraction should cross-check.

fixture.caseTransferChain: a chain of pre-maturity transfers (owner A -> B -> C) keeps maturityHeight byte-identical after each hop -> accept (successor inherits the original maturity height unchanged)
fixture.negativeCompanion: a state transition emitting a later OR earlier maturityHeight after a transfer -> fails
fixture.priorState: settled name, pre-maturity, with an original maturityHeight

inputs.scopeNote: Ratified via #66 (PR-11/12 maturity + X9 no-reset-on-transfer; AUCTION 'Maturity reset on transfer | No' + LAUNCH 'maturity clock does not reset'): a pre-maturity transfer MUST NOT reset or extend the maturity clock; the successor inherits the original maturityHeight unchanged. The near-maturity 'almost-free lock' is the INTENDED, derivable behavior, not a divergence. DELIBERATELY EXCLUDES: S3's bid-height anchor ambiguity (its own spec-blocked item) and the X* transfer-trigger mechanics (cross-checked there, not re-derived here). Authored at candidate tier.
inputs.authoringStatus: authored: predicate-input shape proposed at candidate tier; reviewer sign-off complete; locked. Promoted candidate→ratified 2026-06-15 (#66, DK event 43d30e67) via PR-11/12 (+X9).

expected.invariant: a pre-maturity transfer leaves maturityHeight byte-identical across every hop; the successor inherits the original maturity height; no reset and no extend
expected.rationale: S12 is cited: a pre-maturity transfer MUST NOT reset/extend the maturity clock; the successor inherits the original maturityHeight unchanged (AUCTION 'Maturity reset on transfer | No' + LAUNCH 'maturity clock does not reset'). The no-reset verdict is fully derivable. The flag's substance (buyer near maturity inherits an almost-free lock, X* cross-check, combined-with-S3 shaving) is a corroborating extraction note that re-confirms the no-reset rule, not a verdict divergence; the S3 anchor ambiguity is its own (S3) spec-blocked item, not a defect in S12's verdict.

sources: docs/core/DECISIONS.md 37. Bond opens the auction [ratified] lines 575-598

inputs.attackFlagText: The legacy scheduled-catalog 'unopened' path is explicitly not the launch story — old-model leakage risk if any catalog-driven materialization survives into the kernel (ChatLunatique hunting-list item).

fixture.caseZeroBids: an auction window passing with zero accepted bids -> materializes no NameRecord (reject)
fixture.casePhantomSettled: a transcript asserting phase=settled with no accepted winning bid -> materializes no NameRecord (reject)
fixture.positiveControl: a lot with one qualifying settling winning bid -> materializes ownership
fixture.priorState: an auction lot, possibly carrying legacy catalog/compat state

inputs.scopeNote: Cited-now (LAUNCH Legacy Scheduled-Catalog Compatibility State + ratified DECISIONS #37 'acquired only by (a) an uncontested cheap claim that finalizes, or (b) the winning bond in an auction'): no auction ownership materializes without an actual settled winning bid; a lot that closes/expires or exists only as catalog/compat state with no accepted bid yields no owner. Fail-closed against catalog-driven materialization leakage (a ChatLunatique hunting-list item). #37 is RATIFIED.
inputs.authoringStatus: authored: predicate-input shape proposed; reviewer sign-off complete; locked.

expected.invariant: an auction window with zero accepted bids, or a transcript claiming phase=settled with no accepted winning bid, materializes no NameRecord; ownership materializes only from an actual settled winning bid (or an uncontested cheap claim that finalizes)
expected.rationale: S15 is cited: no auction ownership materializes without an actual settled winning bid; a lot that closes/expires/exists only as catalog/compat state with no accepted bid yields no owner (LAUNCH Legacy Scheduled-Catalog Compatibility State + ratified DECISIONS #37 'acquired only by (a) uncontested cheap claim that finalizes, or (b) the winning bond in an auction'). The fail-closed posture against catalog-driven materialization leakage is derivable now. #37 (bond-opens) is RATIFIED.
